title: ODI Matrix — Other Commercial and Service Industry Machinery Manufacturing (NAICS 333318)

sections.title: Satisfaction Recalibration
sections.content:
Satisfaction scores recalibrated against incumbent technology profiles from JTBD §2.4:

| Incumbent | Share | Key Strengths | Key Weaknesses |
|---|---|---|---|
| Turbine / Paddle-Wheel | 45–55% | Low cost ($5–15), simple | Clogs in 12 mo, ±5%/yr drift, no diagnostics, 1–3 yr life in harsh media |
| Solenoid-Valve Timed Fill | 30–40% | Zero sensor cost | ±10–20% accuracy, zero detection capability |
| Electromagnetic | 3–5% | ±0.5% accuracy, robust | 5–10x cost, needs power, over-specified |
| Ultrasonic (transit-time) | <5% | No moving parts, diagnostics | Air bubble issues, pipe-full required, 3–5x cost |

**Recalibration adjustments (2 of 41):**

| ID | Need | Sat (orig) | Sat (recal) | Rationale |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| N04 | Detect supply failure / empty pipe <5 s | 4 | **3** | Turbine (45–55%): detects zero flow but not empty-pipe specifically; timed-fill (30–40%): zero detection. ~85% of installed base has no dedicated empty-pipe detection. |
| N23 | Field failure rate <0.2% in 10-yr life | 5 | **4** | Turbine (45–55%): 1–3 yr effective life in harsh media, far below 10-yr target. Dominant incumbent fundamentally cannot meet this need. |

All other satisfaction scores retained — JTBD analysis already incorporated incumbent awareness.
All importance scores retained (inherent to the job).

---

sections.title: ODI Entries (sorted by opportunity score)
sections.content:
`opportunity_score = importance + max(importance - satisfaction, 0)`
`is_underserved = importance > 7 AND satisfaction < 5`
`is_overserved = importance < 3 AND satisfaction > 7`

### Highly Underserved (Opp > 15)

| ID | Error Statement | Imp | Sat | Opp | Under | Impact | P.Rel |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| N04 | Minimize time to detect water supply failure or pipe-empty condition to <5 s to prevent dry-run damage to pumps and heating elements | 9 | 3 | **15.00** | true | safety | false |

### Underserved (Opp 12–15)

| ID | Error Statement | Imp | Sat | Opp | Under | Impact | P.Rel |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| N01 | Minimize likelihood of flow reading deviating >±3% in detergent-laden water across 0.5–50 l/min | 9 | 4 | **14.00** | true | reliability | false |
| N10 | Minimize likelihood of turbine rotor clogging from lint/detergent/scale within 12 months | 8 | 2 | **14.00** | true | reliability | true |
| N15b | Minimize undetected water circuit faults invisible to timed-fill dosing | 8 | 2 | **14.00** | true | safety | true |
| N23 | Minimize field failure rate of flow sensing subsystem to <0.2% within 10-yr design life | 9 | 4 | **14.00** | true | reliability | false |
| N02 | Minimize measurement degradation when water contains suspended detergent residues and lint | 8 | 3 | **13.00** | true | reliability | false |
| N06 | Minimize undetected water leaks >50 L/day within machine fluid circuit | 8 | 3 | **13.00** | true | cost | false |
| N08 | Increase ability to measure per-cycle consumption ±2% for pay-per-use billing | 8 | 3 | **13.00** | true | cost | false |
| N12 | Minimize calibration drift >±5%/yr from paddle-wheel bearing wear | 8 | 3 | **13.00** | true | reliability | true |
| N14c | Increase ability to distinguish genuine zero-flow from ultrasonic signal loss | 8 | 3 | **13.00** | true | safety | true |
| N19 | Increase ability to provide real-time per-cycle water consumption for IoT/compliance | 8 | 3 | **13.00** | true | cost | false |
| N22 | Minimize measurement failure when recycled water (TDS 200–2,000 ppm) enters supply | 8 | 3 | **13.00** | true | reliability | false |
| N24 | Increase ability to document measured consumption for WaterSense/Energy Star certification | 8 | 3 | **13.00** | true | cost | false |
| PR-1 | Minimize ultrasonic signal attenuation when air entrainment exceeds 2% vol during commercial wash cycles | 8 | 4 | **12.00** | true | reliability | true |
| N15 | Minimize timed-fill volume deviation >±15% from supply pressure variations | 7 | 2 | **12.00** | false | reliability | true |

### Appropriately Served (Opp 10–12)

| ID | Error Statement | Imp | Sat | Opp | Under | Impact | P.Rel |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| N03 | Minimize repeatability variation >±1% between identical wash cycles | 8 | 5 | **11.00** | false | reliability | false |
| N11 | Minimize frequency of manual turbine sensor disassembly/cleaning | 7 | 3 | **11.00** | false | cost | true |
| N12b | Increase turbine low-flow accuracy below 2 l/min (rotor stall) | 7 | 3 | **11.00** | false | reliability | true |
| N18b | Minimize undetected end-of-line calibration error >±3% | 8 | 5 | **11.00** | false | reliability | false |
| N20 | Increase ability to detect abnormal consumption >20% above baseline | 7 | 3 | **11.00** | false | reliability | false |
| N25 | Increase ability to demonstrate measured water efficiency for eco-label certification | 7 | 3 | **11.00** | false | cost | false |
| N26 | Increase ability to provide per-cycle data on HMI/IoT dashboard for sustainability reporting | 7 | 3 | **11.00** | false | skill | false |
| N27 | Minimize unexpected water utility bill >15% over budget from undetected equipment waste | 7 | 3 | **11.00** | false | cost | false |
| PR-2 | Minimize time to restore valid flow measurement after transient foam/bubble events dissipate | 7 | 3 | **11.00** | false | reliability | true |
| N14 | Minimize signal loss from detergent foam/air bubbles during normal operation | 7 | 4 | **10.00** | false | reliability | true |
| N16b | Minimize mounting-location turbulence causing >2% systematic reading bias | 7 | 4 | **10.00** | false | reliability | false |
| PR-3 | Minimize invalid output during drain-to-fill transitions (partial pipe fill) | 7 | 4 | **10.00** | false | reliability | true |
| PR-4 | Minimize measurement error from <5×DN straight pipe run in compact machine enclosures | 7 | 4 | **10.00** | false | reliability | true |
| PR-5 | Minimize measurement dead zone at trickle-fill below 4.2 l/min (DN20) | 7 | 4 | **10.00** | false | reliability | true |

### Below Threshold (Opp < 10)

| ID | Error Statement | Imp | Sat | Opp | Under | Impact | P.Rel |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| N05 | Increase turndown ratio (0.5–40 l/min) in a single sensor | 7 | 5 | **9.00** | false | reliability | false |
| N07 | Minimize sensor spec mismatch with machine operating conditions | 7 | 5 | **9.00** | false | cost | false |
| N09 | Minimize measurement failure at 5–85°C temperature range | 7 | 5 | **9.00** | false | reliability | false |
| N16 | Minimize installation envelope / straight-pipe clearance requirement | 7 | 5 | **9.00** | false | cost | false |
| N17 | Minimize OEM assembly-line integration time | 7 | 5 | **9.00** | false | time | false |
| N18 | Minimize undetected installation defect passing end-of-line test | 7 | 5 | **9.00** | false | skill | false |
| PR-6 | Minimize transient error during rapid temperature cycling (5–85°C) | 7 | 5 | **9.00** | false | reliability | true |
| N14b | Minimize OEM qualification cost across multiple pipe diameters | 6 | 4 | **8.00** | false | cost | true |
| N21 | Minimize sensor variant count to cover DN12–DN25 range | 6 | 4 | **8.00** | false | cost | false |
| N13 | Minimize commissioning configuration time (ultrasonic) | 6 | 5 | **7.00** | false | time | true |
| PR-7 | Minimize polymer housing seal degradation at 85°C near 90°C limit | 6 | 5 | **7.00** | false | reliability | true |
| PR-8 | Minimize acoustic coupling degradation from surfactant concentration shifts | 6 | 5 | **7.00** | false | reliability | true |

sections.title: Market ODI Summary
sections.content:
| Metric | Value |
|---|---|
| **Market** | Other Commercial and Service Industry Machinery Manufacturing |
| **NAICS** | 333318 |
| **Total Needs** | 41 |
| **Underserved Count** | 14 |
| **Overserved Count** | 0 |
| **Avg Opportunity Score** | 10.98 |

**Top 5 Opportunities:**

| Rank | Opp | Statement |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | 15.00 | Minimize time to detect water supply failure or pipe-empty condition to <5 s to prevent dry-run damage |
| 2 | 14.00 | Minimize likelihood of flow reading deviating >±3% in detergent-laden water |
| 3 | 14.00 | Minimize field failure rate of flow sensing subsystem to <0.2% within 10-yr design life |
| 4 | 14.00 | Minimize likelihood of turbine rotor clogging from lint/detergent/scale within 12 months |
| 5 | 14.00 | Minimize undetected water circuit faults invisible to timed-fill dosing |

**Opportunity distribution:**

| Zone | Count | % |
|---|---|---|
| Highly Underserved (>15) | 1 | 2% |
| Underserved (12–15) | 13 | 32% |
| Appropriately Served (10–12) | 14 | 34% |
| Below Threshold (<10) | 13 | 32% |

---

sections.title: Cross-Check vs Feature-Market Fit (Step 09)
sections.content:
| Metric | Value |
|---|---|
| ODI avg opportunity score | 10.98 |
| Feature-Fit avg (Step 09) | 4.54 |
| ODI avg > 12 AND Fit avg < 4? | **No** (10.98, 4.54) |
| ODI avg < 8 AND Fit avg > 7? | **No** |
| **Consistency result** | No inconsistencies detected |

**Interpretation:** The ODI average of 10.98 sits at the upper boundary of "Appropriately Served," but the distribution is bimodal — 14 needs are underserved (34%) while 13 are below threshold (32%). This market has concentrated, high-value unmet needs around measurement reliability, fault detection, and maintenance elimination, alongside well-served operational integration needs. The Feature-Fit average of 4.54 is moderate, consistent with the ODI picture: the Marquardt ultrasonic sensor addresses the top underserved needs (no moving parts, anomaly detection, accuracy) but has technology-specific limitations (air bubbles, pipe-full condition) that create their own product-related needs.

---

sections.title: QA Checklist
sections.content:
| # | Check | Result | Note |
|---|---|---|---|
| Q1 | Formula correct | **PASS** | Spot-checked N04 (9+max(9-3,0)=15), N10 (8+max(8-2,0)=14), N13 (6+max(6-5,0)=7), PR-5 (7+max(7-4,0)=10), N27 (7+max(7-3,0)=11) — all match |
| Q2 | Underserved flags correct | **PASS** | All 14 entries with imp>7 AND sat<5 flagged; none others |
| Q3 | Overserved flags correct | **PASS** | 0 entries have imp<3 AND sat>7; none flagged |
| Q4 | Need count preserved | **PASS** | 33 from Step 06 + 8 from Step 10 = 41 >= 33 |
| Q5 | Top-5 ordering correct | **PASS** | Sorted by descending opp score; ties broken by importance |
| Q6 | Cross-check ran | **PASS** | ODI vs Feature-Fit checked; no inconsistencies |

---

rows.Key Weaknesses: Clogs in 12 mo, ±5%/yr drift, no diagnostics, 1–3 yr life in harsh media
Air bubble issues, pipe-full required, 3–5x cost

rows.Need: Detect supply failure / empty pipe <5 s
rows.Rationale: Turbine (45–55%): detects zero flow but not empty-pipe specifically; timed-fill (30–40%): zero detection. ~85% of installed base has no dedicated empty-pipe detection.
Turbine (45–55%): 1–3 yr effective life in harsh media, far below 10-yr target. Dominant incumbent fundamentally cannot meet this need.

rows.Error Statement: Minimize time to detect water supply failure or pipe-empty condition to <5 s to prevent dry-run damage to pumps and heating elements
Minimize likelihood of flow reading deviating >±3% in detergent-laden water across 0.5–50 l/min
Minimize likelihood of turbine rotor clogging from lint/detergent/scale within 12 months
Minimize undetected water circuit faults invisible to timed-fill dosing
Minimize field failure rate of flow sensing subsystem to <0.2% within 10-yr design life
Minimize measurement degradation when water contains suspended detergent residues and lint
Minimize undetected water leaks >50 L/day within machine fluid circuit
Increase ability to measure per-cycle consumption ±2% for pay-per-use billing
Minimize calibration drift >±5%/yr from paddle-wheel bearing wear
Increase ability to distinguish genuine zero-flow from ultrasonic signal loss
Increase ability to provide real-time per-cycle water consumption for IoT/compliance
Minimize measurement failure when recycled water (TDS 200–2,000 ppm) enters supply
Increase ability to document measured consumption for WaterSense/Energy Star certification
Minimize ultrasonic signal attenuation when air entrainment exceeds 2% vol during commercial wash cycles
Minimize timed-fill volume deviation >±15% from supply pressure variations
Minimize repeatability variation >±1% between identical wash cycles
Minimize frequency of manual turbine sensor disassembly/cleaning
Increase turbine low-flow accuracy below 2 l/min (rotor stall)
Minimize undetected end-of-line calibration error >±3%
Increase ability to detect abnormal consumption >20% above baseline
Increase ability to demonstrate measured water efficiency for eco-label certification
Increase ability to provide per-cycle data on HMI/IoT dashboard for sustainability reporting
Minimize unexpected water utility bill >15% over budget from undetected equipment waste
Minimize time to restore valid flow measurement after transient foam/bubble events dissipate
Minimize signal loss from detergent foam/air bubbles during normal operation
Minimize mounting-location turbulence causing >2% systematic reading bias
Minimize invalid output during drain-to-fill transitions (partial pipe fill)
Minimize measurement error from <5×DN straight pipe run in compact machine enclosures
Minimize measurement dead zone at trickle-fill below 4.2 l/min (DN20)
Increase turndown ratio (0.5–40 l/min) in a single sensor
Minimize sensor spec mismatch with machine operating conditions
Minimize measurement failure at 5–85°C temperature range
Minimize installation envelope / straight-pipe clearance requirement
Minimize OEM assembly-line integration time
Minimize undetected installation defect passing end-of-line test
Minimize transient error during rapid temperature cycling (5–85°C)
Minimize OEM qualification cost across multiple pipe diameters
Minimize sensor variant count to cover DN12–DN25 range
Minimize commissioning configuration time (ultrasonic)
Minimize polymer housing seal degradation at 85°C near 90°C limit
Minimize acoustic coupling degradation from surfactant concentration shifts

Minimize likelihood of flow reading deviating >±3% in detergent-laden water
